Incremental partial-object decode judged out of scope (needs an event-based JSON parser; revisit only if a real consumer needs it). Documented in docs/streaming.md.

title: SP1: bump crucible GHC 9.6.5 -> 9.10.1
description: Platform bump to align with manifest (9.10.1), prerequisite for sharing an autodocodec types package. Update flake.nix (provide ghc9101 + nixpkgs) and zinc.toml ghc pin; rebuild the vendored closure (effectful, http-client-tls+crypton, retry, mlkem/hpke/ram, etc.) under 9.10.1; bump/forward-pin deps as needed, reusing manifest's working 9.10.1 resolution where shared. No behaviour change; success = build + tests green on 9.10.1. Empirical spike-first (discover breakage before planning fixes).
close_reason: Done, clean. GHC 9.6.5->9.10.1: flake 24.05->24.11 + ghc9101 (manifest's nixpkgs rev), zinc.toml ghc=9.10.1. Zero dependency changes (zinc.lock unchanged); full closure rebuilds, tests + live Anthropic path green on 9.10.1. Merged to master (62de4ed). Gating risk for the autodocodec epic cleared.

title: Adopt autodocodec/aeson for manifest interop (shared typed persistence)
description: Epic: converge crucible's domain codec onto autodocodec (what manifest uses) so crucible types are both prompt/tool-schema sources AND manifest-persistable jsonb. Sub-projects: SP1 GHC 9.6.5->9.10.1 bump; SP2 add aeson+autodocodec deps (reuse manifest pins); SP3 migrate Crucible.Codec/Schema -> autodocodec; SP5 shared-types package + manifest persistence link. SP4 (wire-layer Crucible.Json->aeson) optional/deferred. Driver: ecosystem interop with sibling project manifest (../manifest, GHC 9.10.1, autodocodec+aeson+postgres UoW).
notes: SP1 (GHC 9.10.1) DONE 62de4ed. SP2 (aeson+autodocodec deps) DONE 91d5d74. SP3 (full aeson+autodocodec migration; Crucible.Json+Schema deleted; SP4 folded in) DONE+merged 13a06fa — build/suite/live all green. REMAINING: SP5 — shared-types package + manifest persistence link (crucible types now autodocodec-HasCodec, i.e. manifest-ready; the actual entity/Session persistence is the design-heavy payoff, not yet built). Follow-up: crucible has a Maybe-schema test gap bead.

title: A#4: capture token usage and cost from live Anthropic responses
description: Direction A (productionize live path), sub-project 4 - the quick win. Parse the usage object (input_tokens/output_tokens) from Anthropic message responses and surface it to callers of complete/converse. Decide where it lands: a usage accumulator effect, a return-value field, or a logged side-channel. Spans both the text path (anthropicComplete/postMessages) and the chat path (runChatAnthropic). Needs its own brainstorm -> spec -> plan cycle.
close_reason: Shipped: Crucible.Usage (Usage Monoid + estimateCost), parseUsage, accumulator interpreters runLLMAnthropicUsage/runChatAnthropicUsage, demo. Merged to master (55f3b5b). Live-verified: 1270 tokens summed across a tool loop.

close_reason: Eval harness: Case/Expectation (Exactly/Predicate/Rubric), Score, runEval -> Report (pass-rate/mean/per-case), renderReport; LLM-as-judge is a Codec-backed prompt; exact/predicate/judge scoring green on scripted data

NB: dependency M8/crucible-iln (live Anthropic interpreter) still open — orthogonal to M9; force-closed.

description: Decision tool answer codec via oneOfC; pure reduce :: AgentState -> Decision -> Step; tests.
close_reason: Decision tool/answer unification via decisionCodec (oneOfC); pure total reduce -> Step; decode/round-trip/reduce tests green

title: M8: Anthropic interpreter + recordLLM cassettes
description: runLLMAnthropic (HTTP approach decided here: curl shell-out vs minimal client); recordLLM reinterpret writing cassettes; first live run.
notes: 2026-06-08: M8 BLOCKED by zinc REGRESSION zinc-9ac (P0). http-client-tls cascade fully builds+runs in scratch (httptest5: 58 pkgs, tls manager created). BUT the current zinc (b6f063b) REGRESSED subdir-member library builds: crucible (member packages/crucible) fails GHC-82272 'module cannot be found locally' for ALL its modules — even at its committed 0cfecd5 config. Confirmed regression: backup pre-bxw zinc builds crucible (14 pkgs, BUILD=0); current zinc fails. Minimal repro (any members=[packages/foo] workspace) in zinc-9ac. So: backup zinc builds crucible but lacks cascade support; current zinc has cascade but can't build crucible. NEITHER works for M8 until zinc-9ac is fixed. Crucible zinc config reverted to committed (clean). ONCE zinc-9ac LANDS: rebuild zinc, then redo crucible dep setup (vendor hpke-0.1.0; add http-client+http-client-tls+bytestring to packages/crucible/zinc.toml [build.lib] depends; zinc add http-client-tls), build crucible, then implement runLLMAnthropic (Anthropic messages API via http-client-tls: POST v1/messages, headers x-api-key/anthropic-version/content-type, JSON body from Messages, parse content[0].text with Crucible.Json) + recordLLM cassettes + first live run (.env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Reference cascade resolve: /tmp/httptest5.
close_reason: M8 DONE + verified live. Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.runLLMAnthropic discharges the LLM effect against the real Anthropic Messages API (POST /v1/messages) over http-client-tls; request/response JSON via in-repo Crucible.Json codecs (no aeson). recordLLMAnthropic + runLLMCassette implement the record/replay cassette slider. Crucible.Json.Encode now renders whole numbers as integers (max_tokens). First live run returns 'pong'; cassette replay matches live. Committed on branch m8-anthropic (4d68920); full suite green. Enabled by the http-client-tls cascade now building under zinc (zinc-bxw/y24/ngd/22z/ix4/hz2/mmx/9ac).

title: Skill prompt upgrades from prompt-performance research
description: Implement the recommendations in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-prompt-performance.md: (1) withReasoning combinator wrapping the output codec as {reasoning, result} with reasoning first, (2) few-shot rendering of attached Exactly cases as example blocks via the codecs, (3) prompt tweaks (machine-parse motivation line, input delimiters, trailing format reminder, field docs in schemaText), (4) retry reprompt restating the schema contract, (5) improveSkill testSkill-driven instruction hill-climb (needs a constant/preamble instruction slot).

title: Eval upgrades from rubric research (judge CoT, Checklist, judgeN, calibrate)
description: Implement the recommendations in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-evaluation-rubrics.md: (1) reorder judge JSON to {why, pass} so the verdict conditions on the reasoning, (2) Checklist [Criterion] expectation with weighted binary criteria, (3) judgeN n-vote self-consistency, (4) calibrate helper comparing judge verdicts to hand labels (kappa, fail-class precision/recall), (5) harden judge prompt against verbosity/lean-to-pass bias.
close_reason: Closed

title: OpenAI: streaming + cassette interpreters, live verification

close_reason: Shipped and live-verified: OpenAI.stream/streamChat (SSE event-list parsing, indexed tool-arg reassembly, include_usage), cassette parity via shared Crucible.Chat turn serialization, demo section gated on OPENAI_API_KEY. Live run: typed skill, tool agent, both streams, cassette replay all green.

description: Crucible.Eval (runEval, scoreM, judge, renderReport, LLM-as-judge) and Crucible.Agent/Crucible.Decision (text-path agent loop) are public surface with no manual page. Write docs/evals.md (and decide whether the text-path agent deserves a page or a section).
notes: docs/evals.md now covers the Eval/judge half (shipped with the rubric upgrades). Remaining scope: a page or section for the text-path Agent loop (Crucible.Agent/Decision).

title: Research: techniques to improve prompt performance
description: Research and write up techniques for improving prompt performance (schema-first prompting, few-shot selection, decomposition, self-consistency, reprompting strategies), with recommendations applicable to Skill/prompt and the retry loop. Outcome: written summary.
close_reason: Written up in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-prompt-performance.md: reasoning-field convention, few-shot from attached Cases, prompt/retry tweaks, testSkill-driven optimization loop; follow-up issue filed.

title: Research: techniques to build evaluation rubrics
description: Research and write up (docs or bd notes) practical techniques for constructing LLM evaluation rubrics: rubric decomposition, pairwise vs absolute scoring, judge calibration, known pitfalls. Outcome: written summary with recommendations for crucible's Eval module.
close_reason: Written up in docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-11-evaluation-rubrics.md: binary weighted checklists over Likert, CoT-before-verdict judge, n-vote juries, calibration vs ~30 hand labels; follow-up issue filed for implementation.

title: Test: autodocodec generic schema drops Maybe fields from required
description: SP3 final-review coverage gap. The migration weakened the schema-shape assertions to type==object/array checks; the old tests verified that optional (Maybe) record fields are dropped from a JSON-Schema's 'required' list. No current domain record uses a Maybe field through genericCodec, so this is latent — but before one is added, add a unit test asserting schemaValue of a record with a Maybe field yields a JSON-Schema whose 'required' omits that field (autodocodec's maybeCodec/optional handling). Surfaced reviewing feat/aeson-autodocodec.

title: Streaming: guard against mid-stream idle timeout on long generations
description: The TLS manager's responseTimeout governs time-to-headers, which streaming respects, but a very long streaming generation (e.g. a multi-round tool agent) could in principle stall mid-stream with no per-read/idle timeout. Surfaced in the A#3 SSE-streaming review as an operational follow-up (not a correctness bug). Consider an idle/read timeout on the streamLoop brRead, or document the current behaviour. Low priority until a long-stream stall is actually observed.
close_reason: Shipped: acStreamIdleSecs config (default 60s) + AnthropicStreamTimeout error + timedRead wrapping each brRead in System.Timeout, threaded through streamLoop. Mid-stream stall now aborts (non-retryable) with bracketed responseClose instead of hanging. Merged to master (ae83793).

title: DRY: share request-building + retry between openStream and postMessages
description: openStream (Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.Stream) duplicates ~20 lines from postMessages (Crucible.LLM.Anthropic): the parseRequest+headers+RequestBodyLBS request builder, the recovering retry policy with the AnthropicError handler, and the maxBackoffMicros=30000000 constant. Accepted tradeoff during A#3 (kept the text path untouched + streaming self-contained). Now that openStream/postMessages/anthropicComplete/converseOnce all share the pattern, extract a buildMessagesRequest :: AnthropicConfig -> Value -> IO Request and a shared retry policy/constant into Anthropic.hs and have both call them. Behaviour-preserving refactor.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').

title: Native tool-calling polish: configurable loop cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded
description: Minor follow-ups from the native tool-calling final review. (1) runToolAgent hardcodes defaultMaxIterations (10); make the cap a parameter. (2) ToolLoopExceeded always reports 10 rather than the actual budget used - report the real cap once it is configurable. (3) The loop accumulates full message history with msgs ++ [assistant, results] each round (O(n-squared)); fine at the current bound, revisit only if the cap grows. All non-blocking.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').

title: Chat cassettes: record/replay for the native tool-calling path
description: Deferred non-goal from the native tool-calling spec (2026-06-09). The text path already demos record/replay cassettes; the Chat capability (block-based ChatMsg/Turn, multi-turn tool loops) is richer to serialize and was deferred. Add a recordChatAnthropic + replay interpreter so tool-calling conversations can run hermetically in tests/CI. Pairs with direction C (evals+cassettes in CI).
close_reason: Shipped: turnContentJson (Turn->cassette JSON, round-trips via parseTurn) + recordChatAnthropic/runChatCassette (record/replay for native tool-calling, mirroring the LLM cassettes). Hermetic replay test drives a full runToolAgent tool loop with no network; live demo records+replays a tool-agent run (replay matches). Merged to master (ceb50cf).

title: A#3: streaming (SSE) for the live Anthropic path
description: Direction A, sub-project 3. Consume Anthropic server-sent-events streaming so tokens arrive incrementally instead of one blocking httpLbs call. Touches the shared postMessages HTTP core; likely a new streaming interpreter variant alongside runLLMAnthropic/runChatAnthropic. Larger than A#4. Needs its own brainstorm -> spec -> plan cycle.
close_reason: Shipped: Crucible.Emit (delta effect) + Crucible.LLM.Anthropic.Stream (pure SSE core + runLLMAnthropicStream/runChatAnthropicStream, both returning (a,Usage)). Live-verified: tokens stream live, tool-arg reassembly works, 1271 tokens summed across a streaming tool loop. Merged to master (a51cfd4).

title: Main typed-fn demo prints decode error on live runs
description: In app/Main.hs the typed-fn (llmFn classify) demo prints 'typed fn decode error: expected string, got object' on a successful live API call, because the model replies with surrounding prose/structure rather than a bare word, so the str decoder fails. Pre-existing (predates A#4 usage capture), surfaced during the usage-capture final review. Demo-only cosmetic; fix by tightening the prompt to force a single bare word, or making the demo tolerant. Out of scope for usage capture.
close_reason: Shipped in cleanups bundle, merged to master (72bdd53). 19f: runToolAgentN configurable cap + accurate ToolLoopExceeded. gkw: messagesRequest/withAnthropicRetry shared by postMessages + openStream (behaviour-preserving). 1cb: object-output Sentiment demo round-trips (live: 'typed fn: positive').
